description: Lock touchscreen_interfaces to early touch-display input hardware and exclude later mobile/display/software lineages.

checks.reason: The node remains anchored to Johnson's 1965 touch-display publication.
Electronic sensing and signal handling are direct substrate capabilities for the touch-display device.
Electronics enables the early touch-display substrate without making one electronics lineage the sole possible implementation.
The edited scope is early computer touch-display hardware, not later LCD/mobile panels.
CRT displays preserve the early display-hardware boundary while allowing later touchscreen lineages to use different display substrates.
LCD screens are a later touch-panel substrate and should not be required for the 1965 claim.
MEMS sensors are an anachronistic sensor family for this 1965 touch-display scope.
GUI software is later interaction context, not a prerequisite for early touch-display hardware.
